name: Amore Cashback Card
perks: Cashback on dining and online purchases
bestFor: Everyday grocery and Ayala Malls shoppers
editorVerdict: A solid cashback card for frequent Ayala shoppers. The 4% grocery cashback is among the best in its class, and the first-year fee waiver makes it easy to try risk-free.
pros: Wide BPI branch and ATM network
cons: ₱3,000 annual fee kicks in after year one
Cashback limited to select spending categories
3% monthly interest rate if you carry a balance
eligibilitySummary: Open to Filipino citizens and resident foreigners aged 21–65 with a minimum gross monthly income of ₱15,000. A valid government-issued ID and latest proof of income (payslip or ITR) are required.

faqs.question: How is cashback credited?
faqs.answer: Cashback is automatically credited to your next billing statement. There is no need to manually redeem it.

faqs.question: Can the annual fee be waived after the first year?
faqs.answer: BPI may offer fee waivers if you meet a minimum spend threshold. Check with BPI directly for the latest waiver conditions.

name: Rewards Visa
annualFeeWaiverCondition: No annual fee for life with NAFFL promo
welcomePromo: No Annual Fee for Life (NAFFL) Promo
perks: No annual fee for life under NAFFL promo
Accepted on the global Visa network
bestFor: Everyday shoppers and diners who want flexible rewards
editorVerdict: One of the easiest value-adds in Philippine credit cards — the NAFFL promo removes the biggest psychological barrier to holding a card, and the 3X points on dining and shopping is genuinely useful.
pros: No annual fee for life under NAFFL promo
Points redeemable for cash credits, miles, or merchandise
Available on the Visa network for wide acceptance
cons: Points redemption catalog can be complex to navigate
3% monthly interest rate if you carry a balance
Limited travel-specific perks compared to premium cards
eligibilitySummary: Open to Filipino citizens and resident foreigners aged 21–65 with a minimum gross monthly income of ₱15,000. A valid government-issued ID and proof of income are required. Existing UnionBank account holders may have a faster approval process.

faqs.question: Is the no annual fee for life permanent?
faqs.answer: Yes. Under the NAFFL promo, your annual fee is permanently waived as long as you maintain the card in good standing. If you cancel and reapply later, the waiver may not apply.

faqs.question: How do I redeem my reward points?
faqs.answer: Points can be redeemed via the UnionBank online portal or app for cash credits, airline miles, or items from their rewards catalog. Minimum redemption thresholds apply.
